name: California
description: The Golden State — beaches, Sierras, Hollywood, Silicon Valley, and the country's most populous state.

questions.q: What is the capital of California?

source.notes: State capital since 1854.

questions.q: California's official nickname celebrates which precious metal?

source.notes: Named for the 1849 Gold Rush.

questions.q: Which iconic orange-red bridge spans the entrance to San Francisco Bay?

source.notes: Opened 1937.

questions.q: California is bordered on the west by which ocean?

source.notes: 840 miles of Pacific coastline.

questions.q: Which California theme park, opened in 1955, is often called 'The Happiest Place on Earth'?

source.notes: Located in Anaheim, Orange County.

questions.q: What is California's largest city by population?

source.notes: Roughly 3.8 million residents in the city proper.

questions.q: Which California national park is famous for Half Dome and El Capitan?

source.notes: Designated 1890.

questions.q: California became the 31st U.S. state in what year?

source.notes: Admitted September 9, 1850.

questions.q: Which 1848 event touched off California's largest population boom?

source.notes: Gold found at Sutter's Mill, January 24, 1848.

questions.q: What is the highest mountain in the contiguous United States, located in California's Sierra Nevada?
Which California national park is famous for being one of the hottest places on Earth?

source.notes: Holds the record 134°F reading from 1913.

questions.q: What's the name of the giant alpine lake straddling the California–Nevada border?

source.notes: Second-deepest lake in the U.S.

questions.q: California's famed wine-producing valley, north of San Francisco, is which one?

source.notes: Napa AVA was the first U.S. American Viticultural Area, 1981.

questions.q: Which Spanish Franciscan founded the first California mission in 1769 and 20 more thereafter?

source.notes: Portolá led the land expedition; Serra founded Mission San Diego.

questions.q: Which 1906 disaster devastated one of California's major cities and killed around 3,000 people?
choices: The San Francisco Earthquake and Fire

source.notes: Magnitude ~7.9; fires did most of the damage.

questions.q: Silicon Valley is centered on which Northern California region?

source.notes: South of San Francisco, around San Jose and Palo Alto.

questions.q: Which labor leader co-founded the United Farm Workers and led a famous 1965-70 grape boycott?

source.notes: Chavez and Dolores Huerta co-founded the UFW in 1962.

questions.q: The Spanish explorer who first mapped the California coast in 1542 was whom?

source.notes: Cabrillo entered San Diego Bay in September 1542.

questions.q: Which massive body of salt water, accidentally created in 1905 from irrigation failures, sits in the Imperial Valley?

source.notes: Formed when the Colorado River breached an irrigation canal.

questions.q: Which river forms much of California's eastern border with Arizona?

source.notes: The Colorado marks the California-Arizona border.

questions.q: The short-lived 1846 'Bear Flag Revolt' declared California independent from which nation?

source.notes: Lasted 25 days; U.S. forces took over during the Mexican-American War.

questions.q: The 1869 completion of the First Transcontinental Railroad was celebrated with a Golden Spike ceremony in which U.S. state?

source.notes: Promontory Summit, Utah — the Central Pacific started from Sacramento.
